name: Enterprise

compliance_labels.name: IP Protection
compliance_labels.description: Model weights and fine-tuning data are corporate IP — they must not leave the organization

compliance_labels.name: Data Classification
compliance_labels.description: Customer data, financial data, and trade secrets classified and routed by sensitivity

compliance_labels.name: Audit Trail
compliance_labels.description: Board-level accountability requires a tamper-evident record of every AI decision

compliance_labels.name: Vendor Independence
compliance_labels.description: No single-vendor lock-in — the stack must be portable across infrastructure providers

recommended_models.name: Granite 3B
recommended_models.reason: Apache 2.0, no usage restrictions, fine-tunable on proprietary data

recommended_models.name: Llama 3.2
recommended_models.reason: Strong performance, open weights, broad ecosystem support

key_concern: When your AI runs on someone else's infrastructure, your prompts, your data, and your model's behavior are subject to their terms of service, their jurisdiction, and their business decisions. Enterprise sovereignty means you control the stack.
aibom_requirements: Training data must be classified and approved by data governance team
Model evaluation must include domain-specific benchmarks
Fine-tuning provenance must be documented for audit
Model deployment requires security review sign-off
